_comment: Baseline for ci/check_orphan_cores.py (the wire-or-delete ratchet, DD review §8.2). Every entry is a crate-root `pub mod` with no detected non-test consumer, allowed to stay with the justification given. Adding an entry requires a justification reviewed in the PR that adds it; removing entries (because the module gained a consumer or was deleted) never needs review. Detection is textual — trait-impl seams and feature-gated FFI consumers are its known blind spots, and such entries say so.

allowed_orphans.kirra_map::lanelet2: Feature-gated Lanelet2 cxx FFI bridge (`lanelet2` feature, off by default); its consumer is the ros2+lanelet2 node build, outside the default-feature textual scan.
allowed_orphans.kirra_planner::lanelet2: Same feature-gated Lanelet2 bridge, planner side — consumed only under the `lanelet2` feature build.
allowed_orphans.kirra_trajectory::redundancy_hardening: Verification-hardening module by design: its consumers are the property/hardening test suites; it backs the True-Redundancy cross_check evidence, not a runtime path.
allowed_orphans.kirra_verifier::key_registry: Key-resolution facade (multi-role resolve + verify_ed25519_signature over a stored key). Its sole in-root consumer was the FleetTrustStore impl (src/fleet_trust_store.rs); at the ADR-0035 slice-4 persistence extraction that impl moved into kirra-persistence (orphan rule) and inlined its fleet-role resolution (a base64 decode), leaving KeyRegistry with no in-root consumer. TODO(follow-up): relocate KeyRegistry to kirra-persistence (needs attestation::parse_ed25519_public_pem relocated) or delete if it stays unused — kept this slice to hold the big mechanical move reviewable.
allowed_orphans.kirra_verifier::health: GENUINE ORPHAN (legacy DeploymentLivenessStatus helpers; the live /health route is implemented in the service bin). TODO: wire or delete — kept this cycle to hold the gate's introduction mechanical.
allowed_orphans.kirra_verifier::traceability_gate: CI gate by design: runs as a test lane (SAFETY:/REQ:/TEST: tag coverage); a runtime consumer would be wrong.
allowed_orphans.kirra_verifier::wcet_gate: CI gate by design: the O(1) structural-boundedness WCET guard runs as a test lane; a runtime consumer would be wrong.
allowed_orphans.parko_core::detector: Detection decode path (BBox/Detection/decode_detections) awaiting the real-perception integration (external track, MGA G-4); test-covered, no runtime consumer until sensors land.
allowed_orphans.parko_ros2::image_shim: ROS 2 message-conversion shim awaiting the sensor integration (external track, MGA G-4); consumed by the ros2-feature node build once camera input lands.
allowed_orphans.parko_ros2::odometry_shim: ROS 2 message-conversion shim awaiting the sensor integration (external track, MGA G-4).
allowed_orphans.parko_ros2::radar_shim: ROS 2 message-conversion shim awaiting the sensor integration (external track, MGA G-4).
